title: Helm vs cdk8s for dotCMS customer manifest migration

context.problem: 84 dotCMS customers currently managed via hand-crafted YAML manifests. Need to migrate to a reproducible, declarative approach that enables a provisioner agent to generate configurations from pipeline inputs.
constraints: dotcms/helm-charts v1.0.34 already exists and is maintained
Team has YAML literacy but not deep Helm expertise
Some team members prefer Python-based tooling

rationale.primary: Complexity range (median 31 params, max 74) is squarely in Helm's target zone. Chart already exists, is maintained, and passes 100% semantic equivalence for all 84 customers.

evidence.expressiveness: SUFFICIENT — all 84 customer patterns expressible as values.yaml overrides via myapp.mergeEnvironment deep-merge

helm_pros: Chart already exists and is maintained — zero net-new build cost
ArgoCD native Helm support — no additional tooling
values.yaml is readable by the team (YAML literacy confirmed)
myapp.mergeEnvironment handles per-env overrides without template duplication
100% of 84 customer patterns expressible as values.yaml overrides
Industry-standard tooling with broad operator familiarity
helm_cons: 7 bugs need to be fixed in the chart before migration
affinity.type: preferred not yet expressible (requires BUG-003 fix)
Team needs to learn Helm values structure (low barrier)
Go template syntax in chart is opaque to Python-preferring engineers
cdk8s_pros: Python-native if team prefers Python over YAML
Full programming model for complex conditional logic
cdk8s_cons: Would require building a full Python cdk8s library from scratch
No existing chart to leverage — 100% net-new build
With ArgoCD: generate-and-commit anti-pattern required (no native cdk8s support)
Adds Python/Node.js runtime dependency to the delivery pipeline
No structural advantage for this complexity range
Harder to review: Python code vs YAML diffs in PRs

fluxcd_consideration.question: Would switching from ArgoCD to FluxCD change the decision?
fluxcd_consideration.answer: No. FluxCD has native Helm controller support (equivalent to ArgoCD's). cdk8s would gain nothing structurally — it would still require generate-and-commit to a separate manifest repo. The Helm verdict holds under FluxCD.
fluxcd_consideration.cdk8s_with_fluxcd: Marginally better fit because FluxCD's Kustomize controller could apply generated manifests, but the operational cost of generate-and-commit pipeline complexity still outweighs any benefit at this complexity level.

immediate: Fix 7 documented bugs in dotcms/helm-charts (see gap-analysis/bugs.json)
Use generated values.yaml files as provisioner agent output contract
longterm: Provisioner agent generates values.yaml from pipeline inputs
ArgoCD applies chart + values.yaml per customer environment
Chart team maintains templates; platform team maintains values
Migration path: validate with helm diff, apply via ArgoCD sync
